data.title: Preplanning context file stored in /tmp is environment-specific and lost on session change
data.description:
## Problem

The preplanning skill writes accumulated context to /tmp/preplanning-context-<epic-id>.json (Phase 4 Step 5a). This file is consumed by /dso:implementation-plan when planning individual stories from the epic. However, /tmp/ is:

1. Environment-specific — a worktree session, a different machine, or a container will not have the file
2. Ephemeral — system reboots, tmp cleanup, or session expiration clears it
3. Not versioned — no audit trail of what context was available when implementation planning ran

If preplanning runs in one session and implementation-plan runs in a different session (common for long-lived epics), the context file is missing. The implementation-plan skill silently proceeds without the richer context, producing lower-fidelity plans.

## Expected Behavior

Planning context should be stored with the ticket, not in a local temp file. Options:

1. Store as a ticket comment with a structured marker (e.g., PREPLANNING_CONTEXT: <json>) so it's available in any environment via ticket show
2. Store as a ticket attachment in the event-sourced ticket system
3. Store in a branch-committed file (e.g., .tickets-tracker/context/preplanning-<epic-id>.json on the tickets branch)

Any solution must ensure the context travels with the ticket and is available to any agent in any environment that can read the ticket.

## Impact

- /dso:implementation-plan produces lower-fidelity plans when context file is missing (silent degradation)
- No error or warning surfaced — the skill just proceeds without the enriched context
- Cross-session workflows (preplanning today, implementation-plan tomorrow) always lose context
- Worktree sessions never have the file unless preplanning ran in the same worktree

## Affected Files

- plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md (Phase 4 Step 5a — writes to /tmp/)
- plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md (reads from /tmp/ if available)
